codeReview.summary: The change matches the feature contract: it removes the lossy modulo from the test-owned workspace identifier in TestServicePersistsUserAndWorkspaceExports, restoring per-test workspace isolation under the shared opentoggl_test schema without changing production importing behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker used shell `find`, `grep`, and `cat` commands for repository exploration even though the repo tooling guidance prefers `Glob`, `Grep`, and `Read`. The backend-test-worker skill does not reinforce the repository's exploration-tooling expectations, so workers can drift into lower-signal shell discovery steps.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for ba4b7df1-3156-4744-845a-390efe268a49 includes `find /Users/opentoggl-ci/Code/opentoggl -path "*/importing/application/*_test.go"`, `find /Users/opentoggl-ci/Code/opentoggl -path "*/importing/application/*.go"`, and `cat package.json | grep -A5 '"vp"'` after the initial searches. backend-test-worker SKILL.md lists required files to read and test procedure but does not mention the repo's preferred Read/Grep/Glob-first discovery workflow.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, backend-test-worker skill, and commit ac455a3. The implementation is narrowly scoped and satisfactorily fixes the shared-schema export regression by making the workspace ID fully test-owned and parallel-safe; no blocking or non-blocking code issues were found.
